aliases: Richard T. Crane Medical Prep High School
description: public 4-year medical prep high school on the Near West Side of Chicago, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Richard T. Crane Medical Prep High School (formerly known as Crane Tech Prep or Crane Tech High School) is a public four-year medical prep high school located in the Near West Side neighborhood of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: U.S. Army National Guard training base near Paris, Texas, USA
description_wiki: Camp Maxey is a Texas Military Department training facility that was originally built as a U.S. Army infantry-training camp during World War II. It was occupied from July 1942 to early 1946, and located near the community of Powderly, Texas, in the north-central portion of Lamar County.

description: city in and county seat of Lamar County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Paris is a city in and the county seat of Lamar County, Texas, United States.

description: former US Army post in California
description_wiki: Fort Ord is a former United States Army post on Monterey Bay on the Pacific Ocean coast in California, which closed in 1994 due to Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) action.

description: Virginia Army National Guard installation near Blackstone, Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Fort Pickett, formerly Fort Barfoot, is a Virginia Army National Guard installation, located near the town of Blackstone, Virginia.

description: Largest U.S. Army embarkation camp used during World War II
description_wiki: Camp Shanks was a United States Army installation in the Orangeburg, New York area.

description: cargo ships built in the United States during World War II
description_wiki: Liberty ships are a class of cargo ship built in the United States during World War II under the Emergency Shipbuilding Program.

description: commune in Seine-Maritime, France
description_wiki: Le Havre is a major port city in the Seine-Maritime department in the Normandy region of northern France.

description: military unit
description_wiki: The 67th Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Army during World War I. Organized in mid-1918 at Camp Sheridan, the regiment was preparing to go overseas with the 9th Division when the war ended.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: capital of and state in Austria
description_wiki: Vienna (  vee-EN-ə; German: Wien [viːn] ; Austro-Bavarian: Wean [veɐ̯n]) is the capital, most populous city, and one of the nine states of Austria.

description_wiki: Amherst may refer to:

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: city in Upper Austria
description_wiki: Wels (German pronunciation: [vɛls] ; Central Bavarian: Wös) is a city in Upper Austria, on the Traun River near Linz.

description: capital city of Upper Austria, Austria
description_wiki: Linz ( LIHNTS; Austrian German: [lɪnts] ; Czech: Linec [ˈlɪnɛt͡s]) is the capital of Upper Austria and third-largest city in Austria.

description: palace in Vienna, Austria
description_wiki: Schönbrunn Palace (German: Schloss Schönbrunn [ˈʃlɔs ʃøːnˈbʁʊn] ) was the main summer residence of the Habsburg rulers, located in Hietzing, the 13th district of Vienna.

description: parliament building of the Austrian republic in Vienna
description_wiki: The Austrian Parliament Building (German: Parlamentsgebäude, pronounced [paʁlaˈmɛnt͡sɡəˌbɔɪ̯də]  colloquially das Parlament) in Vienna is the meeting place of the two houses of the Austrian Parliament.

description: opera house in Vienna, Austria
description_wiki: The Vienna State Opera (German: Wiener Staatsoper, IPA: [ˈviːnɐ ˈʃtaːtsˌʔoːpɐ]) is a historic opera house and opera company based in Vienna, Austria.

description: police organization part of the military of a state
United States Army general (1896–1984)
executed American serial killer
description_wiki: Charles Barr (1903 – August 20, 1926), known as The Petting Party Bandit, was an American serial killer who attacked couples at lovers' lanes in Memphis, Tennessee, from January to May 1923, killing three and wounding one.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Switzerland, officially the Swiss Confederation, is a landlocked country located at the intersection of Central, Western, and Southern Europe.

description: city in Austria, eponymous capital city of the federal state of Salzburg
description_wiki: Salzburg is the fourth-largest city in Austria.

description: English playwright, composer, director, actor, and singer (1899–1973)
description_wiki: Sir Noël Peirce Coward (16 December 1899 – 26 March 1973) was an English playwright, composer, director, actor, and singer, known for his wit, flamboyance, and what Time called "a sense of personal style, a combination of cheek and chic, pose and poise".

description: comedy of manners by Noël Coward
description_wiki: Private Lives is a 1930 comedy of manners in three acts by Noël Coward.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

aliases: The Statue of Liberty Enlightening the World
description: American icon in New York, United States
description_wiki: The Statue of Liberty (Liberty Enlightening the World; French: La Liberté éclairant le monde) is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, within New York City.

description: North American trade union
description_wiki: The Graphic Arts International Union (GAIU) was a labor union representing printing workers in the United States and Canada.

description: village in Cook County, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Niles is a village in Cook County, Illinois, United States, located in the townships of Maine and Niles, directly neighboring Chicago's far northwest border.

description: neighborhood in Chicago, Illinois, USA
description_wiki: Humboldt Park is one of the 77 community areas of Chicago in Illinois, United States, located on the West Side of Chicago.

description: ancient Northern Hemisphere spring festival and usually a public holiday
description_wiki: May Day is a European festival of ancient origins marking the beginning of summer, usually celebrated on May 1, around halfway between the Northern Hemisphere's spring equinox and midsummer solstice.
